senators.name: Gary Peters
senators.nerdReason: B.A. magna cum laude (Phi Beta Kappa) from Alma College, M.B.A. from University of Detroit Mercy, J.D. and M.A. in political science from Wayne State University, and M.A. in philosophy from Michigan State University. Five post-secondary degrees — one of the most academically credentialed members of Congress. Navy Reserve Lt. Commander with overseas deployments. 22-year career as financial advisor. Graduate of Naval War College. Combines extraordinary academic breadth with military and financial analytical expertise.
education: M.A. Political Science, Wayne State University
B.A. Political Science (magna cum laude, Phi Beta Kappa), Alma College
experience: Financial Advisor, Merrill Lynch/Paine Webber, 22 years
Griffin Endowed Chair, Central Michigan University

senators.name: Elissa Slotkin
senators.nerdReason: B.A. in rural sociology from Cornell University and M.A. in international affairs from Columbia University SIPA. CIA analyst with three tours in Iraq. Fluent in Arabic and Swahili. Rose to Acting Assistant Secretary of Defense for International Security Affairs. Combines elite academic training with hands-on intelligence analysis and defense policy expertise at the highest levels of government.
experience: Acting Assistant Secretary of Defense for International Security Affairs

MI-01.name: Jack Bergman
MI-01.nerdReason: B.A. from Gustavus Adolphus College and M.B.A. from University of West Florida. Retired U.S. Marine Corps Lieutenant General — the highest-ranking military officer ever elected to Congress. Military career at the three-star general level requires exceptional strategic, analytical, and leadership capabilities across complex global operations.

MI-02.name: John Moolenaar
MI-02.nerdReason: B.S. in chemistry from Hope College and M.P.A. from Harvard University Kennedy School. Worked as a chemist at Dow Chemical Company. Combines genuine STEM credentials (chemistry degree + industrial chemistry experience) with elite public policy training from Harvard. One of the few members of Congress with a hard science degree applied professionally.

MI-03.name: Hillary Scholten
MI-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Calvin College and J.D. from University of Maryland School of Law. Career as immigration attorney at the Department of Justice. Legal training provides analytical skills. Deep expertise in immigration law but approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.
education: J.D., University of Maryland School of Law
experience: Immigration Attorney, U.S. Department of Justice

MI-04.name: Bill Huizenga
MI-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from Calvin College. Career in real estate development and family construction business. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

MI-05.name: Tim Walberg
MI-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Fort Wayne Bible College and M.A. from Wheaton College Graduate School. Career as pastor and state legislator. Religious education and pastoral background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

MI-06.name: Debbie Dingell
MI-06.notNerdReason: B.S. and M.S. from Georgetown University. Career as automotive industry executive (GM) and lobbyist. Deep knowledge of manufacturing, trade, and healthcare policy. Two Georgetown degrees show intellectual commitment. Strong policy expertise but approach is more industry-relationship driven than analytically rigorous.
experience: Wayne State University Board of Governors

MI-07.name: Tom Barrett
MI-07.notNerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point. Army helicopter pilot. Michigan State Senator. West Point education provides analytical discipline but legislative career too new to assess data-driven governance approach.
education: B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

MI-08.name: Kristen McDonald Rivet
MI-08.notNerdReason: B.A. in history from Michigan State University and M.P.A. from University of Michigan-Flint. Career in education and state legislature. MPA provides public administration analytical training. Deep community engagement but approach is more advocacy-oriented than analytically rigorous.

MI-09.name: Lisa McClain
MI-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from Northwood University. Career in financial services. Business background provides practical skills but governance approach is not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.

MI-10.name: John James
MI-10.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and M.B.A. from University of Michigan Ross School of Business. Army Apache helicopter pilot with combat service in Iraq (8 Meritorious Service Medals). West Point provides rigorous analytical foundation. Michigan Ross MBA is a top-tier business school. Combines military strategic thinking with quantitative business analysis. Led family automotive supply company.
education: M.B.A., University of Michigan (Ross School of Business)
B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

MI-11.name: Haley Stevens
MI-11.nerdReason: B.A. and M.A. from American University. Served as Chief of Staff of the Obama Auto Rescue Task Force, coordinating the analytical effort to restructure GM and Chrysler — one of the most data-intensive industrial policy operations in U.S. history. Deep expertise in manufacturing, STEM education, and workforce policy. Brings practical analytical experience from managing a complex government intervention.
experience: Chief of Staff, Obama Auto Rescue Task Force

MI-12.name: Rashida Tlaib
MI-12.notNerdReason: B.A. from Wayne State University and J.D. from Thomas M. Cooley Law School. Career as community organizer and state legislator. First Palestinian-American woman in Congress. Policy driven by community advocacy and social justice rather than analytical or evidence-based frameworks.
education: J.D., Thomas M. Cooley Law School

MI-13.name: Shri Thanedar
MI-13.nerdReason: B.S. and M.S. in chemistry from Indian universities, Ph.D. in polymer chemistry from University of Akron, post-doctoral research at University of Michigan, and M.B.A. from Fontbonne University. Genuine research scientist who built pharmaceutical companies developing life-saving drugs. A Ph.D. in chemistry with a post-doc demonstrates deep research methodology. Entrepreneurial application of scientific expertise to business. Brings rare scientific research background to Congress.
education: Ph.D. Polymer Chemistry, University of Akron
